action_choice_hints: Action cell is in the same row as the agent's current position.
Action cell is the first empty cell encountered in a given direction from the agent.
Action cell is adjacent to a drawn trace segment.
Action cell is within a contiguous run of empty cells bounded by obstacles or traces.
Action cell is not blocked by an obstacle when moving from the agent's position.
Action cell is the nearest cell in a specified direction that is not occupied by an obstacle.
Action cell is within a fixed distance (e.g., within 2 steps) from the agent.
Action cell is in a region containing a special token (e.g., a goal or star) within the same row or column.
Action cell is the only available cell in a segment between two obstacles.
Action cell is at the boundary of a region of empty cells.
Action cell is the first cell after a drawn trace when scanning from the agent's position.
Action cell is in a row or column that contains a special token.
Action cell is not in a region already covered by a drawn trace.
Action cell is in a contiguous segment of empty cells that is longer than a minimum length.
Action cell is the only cell in its row or column not blocked by an obstacle.
Action cell is in a region where all cells between the agent and the action cell are empty.
transition_hints: Selecting an empty cell causes a drawn trace to appear at that cell.
The agent's position does not change after an action.
Obstacles and special tokens remain unchanged after an action.
Drawn traces persist and accumulate with each action.
Selecting a cell already containing a drawn trace has no effect.
Special tokens are not consumed or altered by actions.
Only the selected cell is modified per action; all other cells remain unchanged.
The environment does not automatically advance the agent or any moving tokens.
Drawn traces do not overwrite obstacles or special tokens.
No new obstacles or special tokens are introduced during transitions.

diversity_requirements: Include templates that capture both local (adjacent cell) and global (row/column/region) relations.
Ensure templates cover both proximity-based and alignment-based predicates.
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit the number of templates using fixed distance thresholds to at most 4.
Restrict templates that scan entire rows or columns to at most 3 per direction.
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check for cell emptiness without considering context.
Avoid templates that rely solely on absolute coordinates.
Avoid templates that duplicate predicates differing only by direction unless necessary.
